author_style: Riccardo Carlesso's writing style is professional, informative, and engaging. He often uses humor and personal anecdotes to illustrate his points. His articles are well-researched and provide valuable insights into a variety of topics.

articles_feedback.title: What is toilet paper’s right side?
articles_feedback.summary: The author discusses the different ways to hang toilet paper and provides a detailed explanation of the pros and cons of each method. They also share their own personal preference for hanging toilet paper.

articles_feedback.title: Spaghetti Bolognese don’t exist!!1!
articles_feedback.summary: The author argues that spaghetti Bolognese is not a real Italian dish. They provide several reasons for this, including the fact that the dish is not mentioned in any traditional Italian cookbooks.

articles_feedback.title: Migrate GCP projects across organizations, the gcloud way
articles_feedback.summary: The author provides a detailed guide on how to migrate GCP projects across organizations using the gcloud command-line tool. They also share some tips and tricks to make the process easier.

articles_feedback.title: My favourite Toolchain
articles_feedback.summary: The author shares their favorite toolchain for software development. They discuss the pros and cons of different tools and explain why they prefer certain tools over others.
articles_feedback.song: I've Got a Tool for That

articles_feedback.title: Ruby on Rails-like ORM and scaffolding in Golang anyone?
articles_feedback.summary: The author explores the possibility of creating a Ruby on Rails-like ORM and scaffolding in Golang. They discuss the challenges and benefits of using Golang for this purpose.

articles_feedback.title: My Mac’s battery on Google Cloud Monitoring – with SMS if its low
articles_feedback.summary: The author shows how to monitor their Mac's battery using Google Cloud Monitoring. They also set up SMS alerts to be notified if the battery gets too low.

articles_feedback.title: Canary Deployment on GCP with Cloud Deploy
articles_feedback.summary: The author demonstrates how to perform canary deployments on GCP using Cloud Build and Cloud Deploy. They also discuss the benefits of using canary deployments and how to implement them.

articles_feedback.title: Setting a ◉ Cloud Build trigger with Pulumi in  Python
articles_feedback.summary: The author shows how to set up a Cloud Build trigger using Pulumi in Python. They also provide a code example that you can use to set up your own trigger.

articles_feedback.title: Google Forms to Datastudio
articles_feedback.summary: The author shows how to use Google Forms, Google Spreadsheets, AppsScript, and Datastudio to compare aggregated data into nice graphs.
